data.title: Ma Xingrui under probe - Xinhua…
World food price rise set to continue if Iran war lasts, FAO says
data.description:
PARIS, April 3 (Reuters) - World food prices climbed in March to their highest level since September last year and could rise further if the Middle East conflict that pushed up energy prices continues, the United Nations Food and Agriculture Organization said on Friday.

"Price rises since the conflict began have been modest, driven mainly by higher oil prices and cushioned by ample global cereal supplies," FAO Chief Economist Maximo Torero said in a statement.

But if the conflict lasts over 40 days and input costs remain high, farmers may reduce inputs, plant less, or switch crops to less intensive fertiliser crops, he said.

"Those choices will hit future yields and shape our food supply and commodity prices for the rest of this year and all of the next," he added.

The FAO Food Price Index, which measures changes in a basket of globally traded food commodities, rose by 2.4% from its revised February level. It is 1% above its value a year ago, although nearly 20% below its March 2022 peak, reached after the start of the war in Ukraine.

FERTILISER COSTS COULD LEAD TO REDUCED PLANTING

The cereal price index increased by 1.5% from the previous month, led by a 4.3% increase in international wheat prices due to worsening crop prospects in the U.S. and expectations of lower plantings in Australia due to higher fertiliser costs.

Global maize prices edged up as ample global supply offset concerns over fertiliser costs, and indirect support from greater ethanol demand prospects linked to higher energy prices.

Rice prices dropped 3.0% due to harvest timing and weaker import demand.

Vegetable oil prices increased 5.1%, marking the third consecutive monthly rise. Higher quotations for palm, soy, sunflower, and rapeseed oil reflected the impact of rising global energy prices and expectations of stronger biofuel demand. 

Palm oil prices reached their highest level since mid-2022.

Sugar prices jumped 7.2% in March to their highest since October 2025, as higher crude oil prices drove expectations that Brazil, the world's largest sugar exporter, would channel more sugarcane into producing ethanol.

Meat prices rose 1.0%, led by higher pig meat prices in the European Union and bovine meat prices in Brazil, while poultry prices edged lower.

In a separate report, the FAO slightly raised its estimate for the 2025 global cereal production forecast to a record 3.036 billion metric tons. It would be 5.8% higher year-on-year.

(Reporting by Sybille de La HamaideEditing by Tomasz Janowski and Barbara Lewis)

By Sybille de La Hamaide

IG Metall calls for further easing of combustion engine ban
data.description:
MUNICH/FRANKFURT (dpa-AFX) - In light of the crisis gripping automakers and suppliers, IG Metall chairwoman Christiane Benner is calling for a further relaxation of the combustion engine ban. Her union seeks to secure long-term prospects for employees in the automotive industry, particularly within the supply chain, Benner told "Automobilwoche".

"To achieve this, we need more flexibility than the EU Commission's current draft provides," Benner was quoted as saying. "I would dearly love to pursue a clearer course toward electrification. However, I also see what is happening to jobs in the automotive industry and, above all, in the supply sector."

In December, the EU Commission proposed allowing the registration of new cars with internal combustion engines even after 2035 - a departure from the originally negotiated phase-out. Future exemptions are expected to require only a 90 percent reduction in CO2 emissions compared to the 2021 baseline.

Benner stated that it is now crucial for plug-in hybrids and range-extended vehicles to maintain a fair chance at market share over the coming years. This, she noted, would be of particular benefit to suppliers./isa/DP/zb

Poste Italiane repurchases shares worth over EUR36 million
data.description:
(Alliance News) - Poste Italiane Spa announced that it acquired 1.8 million of its own ordinary shares between March 31 and April 2.

The shares were purchased at an average price of EUR20.5316 per unit, for a total consideration of EUR36.4 million.

With these transactions, the company has concluded the first tranche of its buyback program, while reserving the right to launch further tranches.

To date, the company holds 13.8 million treasury shares, representing 1.1% of its share capital.

By Giuseppe Fabio Ciccomascolo, Alliance News senior reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com

DIHK: Industry sees growing opportunities in defense
data.description:
BERLIN (dpa-AFX) - German industry is increasingly pivoting toward defense production, according to findings from the German Chamber of Commerce and Industry (DIHK). A survey reported by "Wirtschaftswoche" reveals that nearly one-third of surveyed industrial firms see opportunities for their business models within the security and defense sector.

"The survey reflects the strong prospects in the defense industry resulting from geopolitical conflicts," DIHK expert Rainer Kambeck was quoted as saying. In contrast, the broader economic climate remains strained.

According to the DIHK's special analysis, one in six industrial companies in Germany is already integrated into the defense industry value chain. The automotive sector leads the way, with 36 percent of companies active either directly or indirectly in the defense economy.

However, only 2.5 percent of all industrial firms produce explicitly military goods, while 6.9 percent manufacture dual-use goods for both civilian and military applications. An additional 7.6 percent identify as suppliers. Looking ahead, a further 12.3 percent of industrial companies plan to enter the defense sector, adding to the 17 percent currently involved./kf/DP/zb

Ferretti deems takeover bid inadequate as CEO and board clash
data.description:
(Alliance News) - Ferretti Spa has announced that its board of directors considers the increased consideration offered in the public tender for the company's shares to be inadequate, judging the transaction as neither fair nor reasonable for independent shareholders.

The luxury yachting group, dual-listed on Euronext Milan and the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, disclosed that the board  with the abstention of CEO Alberto Galassi and dissenting votes from Piero Ferrari and Stefano Domenicali  approved by majority an update to the response document regarding the offer.

The offer price was recently raised by KKCG to EUR3.90 per share cum dividend, up from EUR3.50. However, the board stated it concurs with the opinion of independent financial advisor Altus Capital, which found the revised price to be inadequate from a financial perspective.

Consequently, the board concluded that the offer, even in light of the updated consideration, is neither fair nor reasonable for independent shareholders  defined as those other than the offeror and parties acting in concert.

Divisions emerged within the independent board committee. The majority of members  Hao Qinggui, Jiang Lan (Lansi), Jin Zhao, Patrick Sun, and Zhu Yi  supported the independent advisor's view, recommending that independent shareholders reject the offer.

In contrast, Piero Ferrari and Stefano Domenicali expressed their dissent, instead urging shareholders to tender their shares.

The updated document includes, among other items, the board's reasoned assessment, the opinion of the Independent Board Committee, and the independent financial advisor's report, all drafted in compliance with Italian regulations and the HK Takeovers Code.

The board specified that its evaluations were based on the terms of the offer published on March 2 and the subsequent supplement on March 26, as well as the analyses provided by independent advisors.

By Giuseppe Fabio Ciccomascolo, Alliance News senior reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2026 Alliance News IS Italian Service Ltd. All rights reserved.
